english: i added: śiṅ-ṅi (shing ngi) of the wood, *yid-di* (*yid di*) of the mind, *bál-li* (*bal li*) of the wool. Note 1. kyi(s) (kyi(s)) when combined with adjective roots, includes the verb to be, e. g. máṅ-gi (mang gi) = máṅ-po yin-gyi (mang po yin gyi). - 2. In colloquial language and later literature the genitive of the verbal root often takes the place of the genit. infinitivi, which seldom occurs in the old classical style, e. g. nam _tsoi (nam 'tsho'i) bár-du (bar du) lifelong. - 3. ji nús kyi(s) (ji nus kyi(s)), ji túb-kyi(s) (ji thub kyi(s)) or vulg. *túb-bi* (*thub bi*), as much as (I, you etc.) can (could etc.) - 4. kyi(s) (kyi(s)), when denoting an antithesis, is often followed by a pleonastical _oṅ-kyaṅ ('ong kyang).
a medic. plant, Gentiana decumbens L., k. dkár-po (k. dkar po) a variety of it with white flowers.
iron hoop Ld.?
a feeling cold, a chill Sch.
the sound of weeping, lamentation.
unburnt brick Sch.
Mil. also kya-s. (kya s.) l., C., an onomatopoetic word: a blowing wind.

used alternatively with gyin (gyin) and gin (gin), after a vowel: yin (yin), denotes a partic. present, e. g. smón-lam _débs-kyin soṅ (smon lam 'debs kyin song) proceed on your way praying! With yod (yod) or _dug ('dug) it forms a periphrastical present tense: smón-lam _débs-kyin yod (smon lam 'debs kyin yod) he is praying (just now): in Ld. even as a real subst.: *śúg-ra _tóṅ-gyin (źig) daṅ* (*shug ra 'thong gyin (zhig) dang*) 'with a whistling sound proceeding from it', *dó-yin-daṅ* (*do yin dang*) 'together with walking' = in walking.
after d, b, s. 1. sign of the instrumentative case, and therefore generally indicating the personal subject of the action. - 2. combined with verbal roots = kyi (kyi).
oh! holla! in calling to somebody; in solemnly addressing a person or an auditory; also merely the sign of the vocative case B. (in W. *wa!* (*wa!*)) kye-kyé (kye kye) id. emphatically.
abbrev. for kye-kyé (kye kye) v. kye (kye).
n. of a bird. Med.

n. of an idol in Lh., consisting like most of the popular idols in those countries of a wooden stick or log decked with rags, but much dreaded and revered; said to be identical with Pe-dkar (pe dkar) in C. Its worship probably dates from a time before Buddhism was introduced.
oh! alas! mostly expressive of sorrow, often combined with kyi-húd (kyi hud); also sign of the vocative case. Seldom it expresses joy. - kye-máo (kye ma'o) id.

upright, erect; *kye-re laṅ-wa* (*kye re lang wa*), resp. *źeṅ-wa* (*zheng wa*) C., kyer-kyér-la dad-će (kyer kyer la dad ce), resp. źaṅ-će* (*zhang ce*) W. to stand; *_go kyer jhé-pa* (*'go kyer jhe pa*) to raise one's head, to look up C. Cf. kyer-ba (kyer ba).
= kyi-húd (kyi hud).
hollo! heigh! well! also like the behold of the Holy Scriptures.
also braṅ-kyéd (brang kyed), with the upper part of the body stretched forward Ld.
v. kye-re (kye re).
hook Sch.
also kyog-kyóg (kyog kyog), kyóg-po (kyog po), crooked, bent, winding, rtse kyog (rtse kyog) with its point bent, crooked at the top. Med.

also kyoṅ-kyóṅ (kyong kyong), kyoṅ-po (kyong po) 1. hard, as e. g. stale bread, ču kyóṅ-po (chu kyong po) hard water; obstinate, unmanageable; kyoṅ-búr (kyong bur) a sort of relievo-work in metal. - 2. oblong Cs. - Sch.: kyoṅ-k̄a (kyong kha) quarrel, kyoṅ-mgó (kyong mgo) cause of a quarrel (?). Cf. gyoṅ (gyong), k̄a-gyoṅ (kha gyong).
